title: DEGgo
text: DEGgo R package end--end bulk RNA-seq differential expression analysis Gene Ontology enrichment. DEGgo provides complete workflow raw count matrices sample metadata biological interpretation: raw count quality control; sample validation filtering; tissue marker verification; differential expression analysis; single pairwise contrasts; Gene Ontology enrichment; extraction genes GO terms interest; publication-ready visualization; automated HTML/PDF reporting.

title: DEGgo
text: package can installed GitHub: Load DEGgo:
code: install.packages("remotes", repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org") remotes::install_github("ymbouamboua/DEGgo") library(DEGgo)

title: DEGgo
text: DEGgo accepts raw count tables matrices. Gene annotation columns can automatically detected using gene_col feature_col.
code: gene_id      gene_name    Sample1    Sample2    Sample3 ENSMUSG1     Adipoq       120        145        98 ENSMUSG2     Lep          65         80         50 ENSMUSG3     Ucp1         12         18         250

title: DEGgo
text: Metadata must contain one row per sample. sample column expected automatically detected using sample_col. pairwise analysis, DEGgo can build comparison variable multiple metadata columns: creates groups :
code: sample      tissue    sex       treatment Sample1     WAT       Female    PBS Sample2     WAT       Female    PAMH Sample3     BAT       Female    PBS pairwise_group_cols = c("treatment", "sex", "tissue") PBS_Female_WAT PAMH_Female_WAT PBS_Male_BAT PAMH_Male_BAT

title: DEGgo
code: qc <- explore_bulk_rnaseq(   counts = counts,   metadata = metadata,   markers = c(     "Adipoq", "Lep", "Ucp1",     "Amh", "Amhr2", "Ar", "Akr1c3"   ),   output_dir = file.path(outdir, "DEGgo_QC_raw") )  qc$qc

title: DEGgo
code: cleaned <- remove_flagged_samples(   counts = counts,   metadata = metadata,   qc_table = qc$qc,   sample_col = "sample",   remove_col = "recommend_remove",   gene_cols = c("gene_id", "gene_name"),   verbose = TRUE )  counts <- cleaned$counts metadata <- cleaned$metadata

title: DEGgo
code: qc_clean <- explore_bulk_rnaseq(   counts = counts,   metadata = metadata,   markers = c(     "Adipoq", "Lep", "Ucp1",     "Amh", "Amhr2", "Ar", "Akr1c3"   ),   output_dir = file.path(outdir, "DEGgo_QC_clean") )  qc_clean$qc

title: DEGgo
code: marker_sets <- list(   BAT = c("Ucp1", "Cidea", "Ppargc1a", "Prdm16", "Dio2", "Elovl3"),   WAT = c("Adipoq", "Lep", "Retn", "Fabp4", "Pparg", "Lpl"),   TESTIS = c("Amh", "Amhr2", "Sox9", "Dhh", "Star", "Cyp11a1", "Cyp17a1", "Insl3"),   OVARY = c("Foxl2", "Fshr", "Cyp19a1", "Bmp15", "Gdf9") )  marker_check <- marker_score_check(   counts = counts,   metadata = metadata,   marker_sets = marker_sets,   sample_col = "sample",   group_col = "tissue",   feature_col = "gene_name" )  marker_check$scores marker_check$swaps marker_check$plot

title: DEGgo
text: Use analysis_mode = "single" one contrast PAMH vs PBS, KO vs WT, treated vs control.
code: results_single <- run_deggo(   counts = counts,   metadata = metadata,   organism = "mouse",   method = "DESeq2",   analysis_mode = "single",   contrast = c("condition", "PAMH", "PBS"),   design_formula = ~ condition,   filter_method = "count",   min_count = 2,   min_samples = 2,   min_total = 10,   generate_report = TRUE,   report_formats = "html",   output_dir = file.path(outdir, "DEGgo_single") )  results_single$summary results_single$report_files

title: DEGgo
text: Use analysis_mode = "pairwise" experiment contains multiple structured comparisons, treatment sex tissue.
code: pairwise_contrasts <- list(   WAT_Female_PAMH_vs_PBS = c("comparison_group", "PAMH_Female_WAT", "PBS_Female_WAT"),   WAT_Male_PAMH_vs_PBS = c("comparison_group", "PAMH_Male_WAT", "PBS_Male_WAT"),   BAT_Female_PAMH_vs_PBS = c("comparison_group", "PAMH_Female_BAT", "PBS_Female_BAT"),   BAT_Male_PAMH_vs_PBS = c("comparison_group", "PAMH_Male_BAT", "PBS_Male_BAT"),   Ovary_PAMH_vs_PBS = c("comparison_group", "PAMH_Female_OVARY", "PBS_Female_OVARY"),   Testis_PAMH_vs_PBS = c("comparison_group", "PAMH_Male_TESTIS", "PBS_Male_TESTIS") ) results <- run_deggo(   counts = counts,   metadata = metadata,   organism = "mouse",   method = "DESeq2",   analysis_mode = "pairwise",   pairwise_group_cols = c("treatment", "sex", "tissue"),   pairwise_contrast_col = "comparison_group",   pairwise_contrasts = pairwise_contrasts,   filter_method = "count",   min_count = 2,   min_samples = 2,   min_total = 10,   generate_report = TRUE,   report_formats = "html",   output_dir = outdir )  results$summary results$report_files

title: DEGgo
text: DEGgo can automatically generate HTML PDF report end run_deggo(). PDF generation requires LaTeX installation, example TinyTeX.
code: report_files <- generate_deggo_report(   results = results,   output_dir = results$output_dir,   formats = c("html", "pdf") )  report_files install.packages("tinytex", repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org") tinytex::install_tinytex()

title: DEGgo
code: expr_df <- extract_expression(   dds = results$dds,   metadata = results$metadata,   genes = c("Adipoq", "Lep", "Ucp1", "Amh", "Amhr2"),   assay = "vst",   gene_col = "SYMBOL" )  head(expr_df) plot_gene_expression(   expr_df,   gene = "Adipoq",   x = "treatment",   color = "treatment",   facet = "tissue",   geom = "violin" )

title: DEGgo
code: go <- run_go_enrichment(   sig_deg = results$sig_deg$WAT_Female_PAMH_vs_PBS,   comparison = "WAT_Female_PAMH_vs_PBS",   ontology = "BP",   orgdb = org.Mm.eg.db::org.Mm.eg.db,   output_dir = file.path(outdir, "GO_WAT_Female_PAMH_vs_PBS") )  go$go_results go$go_plot plot_go_terms(   go_df = go$go_results,   comparison = "WAT Female PAMH vs PBS",   top_n = 15 )

what: Extract genes and GO terms of interest
title: DEGgo
code: genes_interest <- c(   "Amh", "Amhr2", "Ar", "Akr1c3",   "Star", "Cyp11a1", "Cyp17a1", "Cyp19a1",   "Foxl2", "Fshr", "Sox9", "Insl3",   "Pparg", "Fabp4", "Adipoq", "Lep",   "Ucp1", "Prdm16", "Ppargc1a", "Cidea" ) gene_summary <- deggo_extract_deg_genes(results, genes_interest) gene_summary go_keywords <- c(   "tgf", "bmp", "smad", "inflammation", "immune",   "steroidogenesis", "androgen", "steroid",   "adipocyte", "lipid", "fatty acid",   "thermogenesis", "mitochond", "hormone",   "reproductive", "gonad", "ovary", "testis" )  go_keywords_results <- deggo_extract_go_keywords(   results,   keywords = go_keywords )  go_keywords_results

title: DEGgo
code: DEGgo_results/ └── DEGgo_YYYY_MM_DD_DESeq2_pairwise/     ├── pairwise_DESeq2_results/     ├── pairwise_DESeq2_significant/     ├── pairwise_volcano/     ├── pairwise_heatmaps/     ├── pairwise_PCA/     ├── pairwise_GO/     ├── pairwise_GO_plots/     ├── DEGgo_Report.html     ├── DEGgo_Report.pdf     ├── pairwise_summary.tsv     ├── session_info.txt     └── DEGgo_results.rds

title: DEGgo
text: Users can analyze additional organisms supplying Bioconductor-compatible OrgDb object orgdb argument.
code: library(org.Custom.eg.db)  results <- run_deggo(   counts = counts,   metadata = metadata,   organism = "custom",   orgdb = org.Custom.eg.db )

title: DEGgo
text: DEGgo implemented entirely R compatible Linux, macOS, Windows. can used local computers, HPC clusters, containerized environments Docker Apptainer/Singularity.

title: DEGgo

title: DEGgo
text: Overview Workflow Installation Quick start Main functions Supported organisms Documentation Citation License

title: DEGgo
text: DEGgo R package automated bulk RNA-seq downstream analysis. provides end--end workflow raw count matrices sample metadata : quality control; sample validation; differential expression analysis; Gene Ontology enrichment; publication-ready visualizations; automated HTML/PDF reporting. DEGgo supports DESeq2, edgeR, limma designed bioinformaticians experimental biologists.

title: DEGgo
code: install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("ymbouamboua/DEGgo") library(DEGgo)

title: DEGgo
code: results <- run_deggo(   counts = counts,   metadata = metadata,   organism = "mouse",   method = "DESeq2",   analysis_mode = "single",   contrast = c("condition", "treated", "control") )  results$summary

title: DEGgo
text: DEGgo provides built-annotation support following organisms: Custom organisms supported user-supplied Bioconductor OrgDb annotation databases.

code: browseVignettes("DEGgo")

what: Explore and QC bulk RNA-seq count data — explore_bulk_rnaseq
title: Explore and QC bulk RNA-seq count data — explore_bulk_rnaseq
text: Generates pre-differential-expression quality control plots metrics bulk RNA-seq count data, including library size, detected genes, expression distribution, density curves, sample correlation, hierarchical clustering, PCA plots, top variable gene heatmap, optional marker heatmap, automatic sample QC flags.

title: Explore and QC bulk RNA-seq count data — explore_bulk_rnaseq
code: explore_bulk_rnaseq(   counts,   metadata,   gene_col = c("gene_id", "GeneID", "gene", "Gene", "ENSEMBL", "ensembl", "ensembl_id"),   feature_col = c("gene_name", "SYMBOL", "symbol", "gene_symbol", "external_gene_name"),   sample_col = c("sample", "Sample", "SAMPLE"),   annotation_cols = c("condition", "treatment", "sex", "tissue"),   color_by = NULL,   markers = c("Adipoq", "Lep", "Ucp1", "Amh", "Amhr2", "Ar", "Akr1c3"),   top_variable_genes = 500,   output_dir = "DEGgo_QC",   width = 8,   height = 6,   dpi = 300 )

title: Explore and QC bulk RNA-seq count data — explore_bulk_rnaseq
text: counts Count table matrix. Genes rows samples columns. counts data frame, one column must contain gene identifiers. metadata Sample metadata data frame. gene_col Candidate gene identifier columns counts. feature_col Candidate gene symbol/name columns counts. sample_col Candidate sample identifier columns metadata. annotation_cols Metadata columns shown annotations heatmaps. color_by Metadata column used color library size density plots. NULL, DEGgo tries tissue, condition, treatment, sex. markers Optional marker genes marker heatmap. top_variable_genes Number top variable genes heatmap. output_dir Output directory. width Plot width inches. height Plot height inches. dpi Plot resolution.

title: Explore and QC bulk RNA-seq count data — explore_bulk_rnaseq
text: Invisibly returns list containing QC table, plots, correlation matrix, PCA plots, top variable genes, marker heatmap object, output path.

title: Explore and QC bulk RNA-seq count data — explore_bulk_rnaseq
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ qc <- explore_bulk_rnaseq(counts, metadata)  cleaned <- remove_flagged_samples(   counts = counts,   metadata = metadata,   qc_table = qc$qc )  counts <- cleaned$counts metadata <- cleaned$metadata } # }

title: Extract normalized gene expression — extract_expression
text: Extract normalized variance-stabilized expression values DESeq2 object return long-format table.

title: Extract normalized gene expression — extract_expression
code: extract_expression(   dds,   metadata,   genes,   assay = c("vst", "normalized", "log2_normalized", "raw"),   gene_col = "SYMBOL",   collapse_fun = sum )

title: Extract normalized gene expression — extract_expression
text: dds DESeqDataSet object. metadata Sample metadata. genes Character vector gene symbols identifiers. assay One "vst", "normalized", "log2_normalized" "raw". gene_col Character. Column containing gene names identifiers rowData(dds). collapse_fun Function used collapse duplicated gene rows.

title: Extract normalized gene expression — extract_expression
text: Long-format expression table.

title: Generate DEGgo HTML/PDF Report — generate_deggo_report
text: Creates DEGgo analysis report results generated run_deggo().

title: Generate DEGgo HTML/PDF Report — generate_deggo_report
code: generate_deggo_report(   results,   output_dir = results$output_dir,   formats = "html",   report_template = NULL )

title: Generate DEGgo HTML/PDF Report — generate_deggo_report
text: results DEGgo results object returned run_deggo(). output_dir Output directory. formats Character vector. Supported values: "html" "pdf". report_template Optional path custom R Markdown template.

title: Generate DEGgo HTML/PDF Report — generate_deggo_report
text: Invisibly returns generated report paths.

what: Validate sample identity using marker gene signatures — marker_score_check
title: Validate sample identity using marker gene signatures — marker_score_check
text: Computes signature scores predefined marker gene sets, predicts likely sample identity, flags potential sample swaps annotation mismatches.

title: Validate sample identity using marker gene signatures — marker_score_check
code: marker_score_check(   counts,   metadata,   marker_sets,   sample_col = "sample",   group_col = "tissue",   feature_col = "gene_name",   log_transform = TRUE,   plot = TRUE )

title: Validate sample identity using marker gene signatures — marker_score_check
text: counts data frame matrix containing gene expression counts. Rows correspond genes columns correspond samples. metadata data frame containing sample metadata. marker_sets Named list marker gene vectors. list element defines biological group tissue signature. sample_col Column name metadata containing sample IDs. Default "sample". group_col Column name metadata containing expected sample annotation (e.g. tissue, condition). Default "tissue". feature_col Column name counts containing gene symbols. Default "gene_name". log_transform Logical; TRUE, computes scores log2(count + 1) transformed values. Default TRUE. plot Logical; TRUE, returns barplot marker scores. Default TRUE.

title: Validate sample identity using marker gene signatures — marker_score_check
text: list containing: scores Data frame marker scores, predicted groups, swap flags. long Long-format version score table. swaps Subset samples predicted expected groups differ. plot ggplot object showing marker scores per sample plot = TRUE; otherwise NULL.

title: Validate sample identity using marker gene signatures — marker_score_check
text: marker set, function calculates mean expression detected marker genes across samples. predicted group corresponds marker set highest score. function performs simple signature-based classification: Matches marker genes feature column. Computes mean expression matched genes sample. Predicts group highest signature score. Flags samples predicted expected annotations differ. Gene matching case-insensitive.

title: Validate sample identity using marker gene signatures — marker_score_check
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ marker_sets <- list(   BAT = c("Ucp1", "Cidea", "Ppargc1a"),   WAT = c("Adipoq", "Lep", "Fabp4"),   TESTIS = c("Amh", "Sox9", "Ddx4"),   OVARY = c("Foxl2", "Fshr", "Bmp15") )  res <- marker_score_check(   counts = counts,   metadata = metadata,   marker_sets = marker_sets,   sample_col = "sample",   group_col = "tissue" )  head(res$scores) res$swaps print(res$plot) } # }

what: Plot GO enrichment terms for all pairwise comparisons — plot_all_go_terms
title: Plot GO enrichment terms for all pairwise comparisons — plot_all_go_terms
text: Generates GO enrichment plots every comparison returned run_deggo() optionally exports figures disk.

title: Plot GO enrichment terms for all pairwise comparisons — plot_all_go_terms
code: plot_all_go_terms(   results,   top_n = 10,   font_size = 8,   output_dir = NULL,   width = 8,   height = 6,   dpi = 300 )

title: Plot GO enrichment terms for all pairwise comparisons — plot_all_go_terms
text: results DEGgo results object returned run_deggo(). top_n Number GO terms display per regulation class (). Default 10. font_size Base font size used plots. Default 8. output_dir Optional output directory exported figures. NULL, plots returned saved. width Plot width inches. Default 8. height Plot height inches. Default 6. dpi Plot resolution. Default 300.

title: Plot GO enrichment terms for all pairwise comparisons — plot_all_go_terms
text: named list ggplot objects, one per comparison.

title: Plot GO enrichment terms for all pairwise comparisons — plot_all_go_terms
text: function iterates pairwise comparisons stored results$go_results calls plot_go_terms() GO enrichment table. output_dir supplied, PNG PDF versions plots automatically exported.

title: Plot GO enrichment terms for all pairwise comparisons — plot_all_go_terms
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{  go_plots <- plot_all_go_terms(   results = results,   top_n = 10,   font_size = 8,   output_dir = "GO_plots" )  names(go_plots)  go_plots$WAT_Female_PAMH_vs_PBS  } # }

title: Plot normalized gene expression — plot_gene_expression
text: Generates publication-ready violin, boxplot, barplot visualizations normalized expression values produced extract_normalized_expression().

title: Plot normalized gene expression — plot_gene_expression
code: plot_gene_expression(   expr_df,   gene,   x = "treatment",   color = x,   facet = NULL,   geom = c("violin", "boxplot", "barplot"),   comparisons = list(c("PBS", "PAMH")),   stats = TRUE,   stat_method = "wilcox.test",   stat_label = c("p.signif", "p.format"),   output_dir = NULL,   filename = NULL,   width = 6,   height = 5,   dpi = 300,   txtsize = 12,   x_ang = 45,   ncol = NULL )

title: Plot normalized gene expression — plot_gene_expression
text: expr_df Long-format expression table. gene Gene symbol plot. x Metadata variable displayed x-axis. color Metadata variable used fill colors. facet Optional metadata variable used faceting. geom Plot type: "violin", "boxplot", "barplot". comparisons List comparisons passed ggpubr::stat_compare_means(). stats Logical. Add statistical comparisons. stat_method Statistical test method. stat_label Label style: "p.signif" "p.format". output_dir Output directory. filename Output filename. width Plot width. height Plot height. dpi Plot resolution. txtsize Base font size. x_ang Numeric. Angle x-axis labels. ncol Number plot columns.

title: Plot normalized gene expression — plot_gene_expression
text: ggplot object.

title: Plot normalized gene expression — plot_gene_expression
text: Statistical comparisons can added using ggpubr::stat_compare_means().

title: Plot normalized gene expression — plot_gene_expression
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ plot_gene_expression(   expr_df,   gene = "Adipoq",   x = "treatment",   color = "treatment",   facet = "tissue",   geom = "violin" ) } # }

what: Plot expression heatmap for selected genes — plot_gene_heatmap
title: Plot expression heatmap for selected genes — plot_gene_heatmap

title: Plot expression heatmap for selected genes — plot_gene_heatmap
code: plot_gene_heatmap(   counts,   metadata,   genes,   gene_col = c("gene_id", "GeneID", "gene", "Gene", "ENSEMBL", "ensembl", "ensembl_id"),   feature_col = c("gene_name", "SYMBOL", "symbol", "gene_symbol", "external_gene_name"),   sample_col = c("sample", "Sample", "SAMPLE"),   assay_transform = c("log2", "log2cpm"),   annotation_cols = c("condition", "treatment", "sex", "tissue"),   annotation_colors = NULL,   order_by = NULL,   output_dir = "DEGgo_out",   filename = "Gene_Expression_Heatmap",   main = "Selected gene expression heatmap",   color = (grDevices::colorRampPalette(c("#6497b1", "#F7F7F7", "#740001")))(100),   breaks = seq(-2, 2, length.out = 101),   border_color = "#9C9391",   scale_rows = TRUE,   cluster_rows = TRUE,   cluster_cols = FALSE,   fontsize_row = 8,   fontsize_col = 7,   width = 10,   height = 7 )

title: Plot expression heatmap for selected genes — plot_gene_heatmap
text: counts Count matrix count table. metadata Sample metadata. genes Genes display. gene_col Gene ID column. feature_col Gene symbol column. sample_col Sample column. assay_transform Expression transformation. annotation_cols Metadata columns shown heatmap. annotation_colors Named list annotation colors. order_by Metadata columns used order samples. output_dir Output directory. filename Output filename. main Heatmap title. color Heatmap color palette. breaks Numeric vector color breaks. border_color Border color heatmap cells. scale_rows Scale genes. cluster_rows Cluster genes. cluster_cols Cluster samples. fontsize_row Row names size. fontsize_col Column names size. width Plot width. height Plot height.

title: Generate DEGgo heatmap — plot_heatmap
text: Generates clustered heatmap top differentially expressed genes.

title: Generate DEGgo heatmap — plot_heatmap
code: plot_heatmap(   vsd,   res_df,   metadata,   contrast = NULL,   sample_subset = NULL,   metadata_filter = NULL,   top_n_heatmap = 50,   padj_cutoff = 0.05,   main = "Top Differentially Expressed Genes",   output_dir = "DEGgo_out",   filename = "Heatmap",   fallback = TRUE,   annotation_cols = c("condition", "treatment", "sex", "tissue"),   annotation_colors = NULL,   order_by = NULL,   scale_rows = TRUE,   cluster_rows = TRUE,   cluster_cols = FALSE,   fontsize_row = 8,   fontsize_col = 7,   width = 8,   height = 10 )

title: Generate DEGgo heatmap — plot_heatmap
text: vsd Variance-stabilized dataset. res_df Differential expression results data frame. metadata Sample metadata data frame. contrast Contrast name plot. sample_subset Optional sample vector retain. metadata_filter Optional named list used filter metadata. top_n_heatmap Number genes displayed heatmap. padj_cutoff Adjusted p-value threshold. main Heatmap title. output_dir Directory exported plots. filename Output file name without extension. fallback Logical. TRUE, use top ranked genes significant genes pass padj_cutoff. annotation_cols Metadata columns shown heatmap annotations. annotation_colors Named list annotation colors. order_by Metadata columns used order samples. scale_rows Logical; scale rows plotting. cluster_rows Logical; cluster genes. cluster_cols Logical; cluster samples. fontsize_row Row label font size. fontsize_col Column label font size. width Plot width inches. height Plot height inches.

title: Generate DEGgo heatmap — plot_heatmap
text: Heatmap expression matrix, invisibly.

title: Generate DEGgo PCA plot — plot_pca
text: Generate DEGgo PCA plot

title: Generate DEGgo PCA plot — plot_pca
code: plot_pca(   dds,   metadata,   output_dir = "DEGgo_out",   filename = "PCA_Plot",   intgroup = "condition",   color_by = NULL,   shape_by = NULL,   title = "PCA Plot",   width = 8,   height = 6,   dpi = 300 )

title: Generate DEGgo PCA plot — plot_pca
text: dds DESeq2 dataset. metadata Sample metadata. output_dir Output directory. filename Output filename without extension. intgroup Metadata columns used PCA coloring/grouping. color_by Metadata column used point color. shape_by Metadata column used point shape. title Plot title. width Plot width inches. height Plot height inches. dpi Plot resolution.

title: Generate DEGgo PCA plot — plot_pca
text: list containing VST object, PCA data, plot.

what: Generate volcano plot for differential expression results — plot_volcano
title: Generate volcano plot for differential expression results — plot_volcano
text: Creates customizable volcano plot DEGgo differential expression results. Genes automatically classified , , Ns based log2 fold-change p-value thresholds.

title: Generate volcano plot for differential expression results — plot_volcano
code: plot_volcano(   res_df,   gene_col = NULL,   lfc_col = "log2FoldChange",   pval_col = "pvalue",   padj_col = "padj",   use_padj = TRUE,   logfc_cutoff = 0.25,   padj_cutoff = 0.05,   top_n_labels = 40,   genes_highlight = NULL,   colors = c(Up = "#740001", Down = "#6497b1", Ns = "gray70"),   point_size = 1.5,   alpha = 0.65,   title = "Volcano Plot",   legend = TRUE,   output_dir = "DEGgo_out",   filename = "Volcano_Plot",   save_pdf = TRUE,   width = 10,   height = 8,   dpi = 300,   style = "classic",   txtsize = 12 )

title: Generate volcano plot for differential expression results — plot_volcano
text: res_df Differential expression result data frame. gene_col Column containing gene labels. unavailable, function automatically tries "SYMBOL", "gene", "ENSEMBL". lfc_col Column containing log2 fold-change values. pval_col Column containing raw p-values. padj_col Column containing adjusted p-values. use_padj Logical. TRUE, use padj_col; otherwise use pval_col. logfc_cutoff Absolute log2 fold-change threshold. padj_cutoff P-value adjusted p-value threshold. top_n_labels Number top significant genes label. genes_highlight Optional character vector genes highlight. colors Named vector colors , , Ns. point_size Point size. alpha Point transparency. title Plot title. legend Logical. Show legend. output_dir Output directory. NULL, plot saved. filename Output file name without extension. save_pdf Logical. Also save PDF version. width Plot width. height Plot height. dpi PNG resolution. style Plot style passed .deggo_theme(). txtsize Base text size.

title: Generate volcano plot for differential expression results — plot_volcano
text: ggplot object.

what: Remove flagged samples from counts and metadata — remove_flagged_samples
title: Remove flagged samples from counts and metadata — remove_flagged_samples
text: Removes samples flagged exclusion QC table returns synchronized counts metadata objects.

title: Remove flagged samples from counts and metadata — remove_flagged_samples
code: remove_flagged_samples(   counts,   metadata,   qc_table,   sample_col = "sample_original",   remove_col = "recommend_remove",   gene_cols = c("gene_id", "gene_name"),   verbose = TRUE )

title: Remove flagged samples from counts and metadata — remove_flagged_samples
text: counts Count matrix data frame. Rows genes columns samples. Gene annotation columns preserved. metadata Sample metadata. qc_table QC table containing sample identifiers removal flags. sample_col Column containing sample names. Default "sample_original". remove_col Logical column indicating samples remove. Default "recommend_remove". gene_cols Character vector annotation columns preserve. Default c("gene_id", "gene_name"). verbose Logical; print summary messages. Default TRUE.

title: Remove flagged samples from counts and metadata — remove_flagged_samples
text: list counts, metadata, removed sample names.

title: Remove flagged samples from counts and metadata — remove_flagged_samples
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ qc <- explore_bulk_rnaseq(counts, metadata)  cleaned <- remove_flagged_samples(   counts = counts,   metadata = metadata,   qc_table = qc$qc )  counts <- cleaned$counts metadata <- cleaned$metadata } # }

what: Run DEGgo bulk RNA-seq downstream analysis — run_deggo
title: Run DEGgo bulk RNA-seq downstream analysis — run_deggo
text: Run automated bulk RNA-seq downstream analysis workflow including input preparation, sample matching, differential expression analysis, result annotation, visualization, heatmap generation, PCA analysis, Gene Ontology enrichment, optional HTML/PDF report generation.

title: Run DEGgo bulk RNA-seq downstream analysis — run_deggo
code: run_deggo(   counts,   metadata,   gene_col = c("gene_id", "GeneID", "gene", "Gene", "ENSEMBL", "ensembl", "ensembl_id"),   feature_col = c("gene_name", "SYMBOL", "symbol", "gene_symbol", "external_gene_name"),   sample_col = c("sample", "Sample", "SAMPLE"),   prepare_input = TRUE,   output_dir = NULL,   padj_cutoff = 0.05,   logfc_cutoff = 0.25,   top_n_heatmap = 50,   top_n_labels = 10,   ontology = c("BP", "MF", "CC"),   organism = c("human", "mouse", "rat", "custom"),   orgdb = NULL,   method = c("DESeq2", "edgeR", "limma"),   analysis_mode = c("single", "pairwise"),   contrast = NULL,   design_formula = ~condition,   pairwise_group_cols = NULL,   pairwise_contrast_col = "comparison_group",   pairwise_contrasts = NULL,   filter_method = c("count", "cpm", "none"),   pairwise_auto = TRUE,   pairwise_mode = c("all", "within_first", "within_second"),   min_count = 5,   min_samples = 2,   min_total = 10,   generate_report = TRUE,   report_formats = "html",   report_template = NULL,   save_reproducibility = TRUE,   save_clean_inputs = TRUE,   seed = 123 )

title: Run DEGgo bulk RNA-seq downstream analysis — run_deggo
text: counts Raw count table matrix. metadata Sample metadata data frame. gene_col Character vector possible gene identifier columns. feature_col Character vector possible feature annotation columns. sample_col Character vector possible sample identifier columns. prepare_input Logical. TRUE, automatically matches counts metadata. output_dir Output directory. NULL, dated directory created. padj_cutoff Adjusted p-value cutoff. logfc_cutoff Absolute log2 fold-change cutoff. top_n_heatmap Number top genes heatmap. top_n_labels Number top genes labeled volcano plot. ontology GO ontology: BP, MF, CC. organism Organism: human, mouse, rat custom user. orgdb Optional OrgDb object. method DE method: DESeq2, edgeR, limma. analysis_mode Analysis mode: single pairwise. contrast Optional contrast single analysis. design_formula Design formula. pairwise_group_cols Metadata columns pairwise groups. pairwise_contrast_col Name pairwise contrast column. pairwise_contrasts Optional named list contrasts. filter_method Filtering method: count, cpm, none. pairwise_auto Automatically generate pairwise contrasts. pairwise_mode Pairwise mode: , within_first, within_second. min_count Minimum count filtering. min_samples Minimum samples filtering. min_total Minimum total count filtering. generate_report Logical. Generate DEGgo report. report_formats Character vector. Report formats: html /pdf. report_template Optional path report R Markdown template. save_reproducibility Logical. Save run parameters reproducibility files. save_clean_inputs Logical. Save cleaned counts matched metadata. seed Random seed.

title: Run DEGgo bulk RNA-seq downstream analysis — run_deggo
text: list containing DEG results, plots, GO results, reports, output paths.

what: Run Gene Ontology enrichment analysis — run_go_enrichment
title: Run Gene Ontology enrichment analysis — run_go_enrichment
text: Performs Gene Ontology enrichment analysis separately upregulated downregulated significant differentially expressed genes using Entrez identifiers.

title: Run Gene Ontology enrichment analysis — run_go_enrichment
code: run_go_enrichment(   sig_deg,   comparison = NULL,   ontology = c("BP", "MF", "CC"),   orgdb,   output_dir = "DEGgo_out",   pvalue_cutoff = 0.05,   qvalue_cutoff = 0.2,   p_adjust_method = "BH",   logfc_col = "log2FoldChange",   entrez_col = "ENTREZID",   min_genes = 10 )

title: Run Gene Ontology enrichment analysis — run_go_enrichment
text: sig_deg Significant DEG data frame containing Entrez IDs log2 fold-change values. comparison Character. Comparison name used label GO enrichment results exported files. ontology Character. GO ontology test. One "BP", "MF" "CC". orgdb OrgDb annotation database used clusterProfiler::enrichGO(). output_dir Character. Output directory. pvalue_cutoff Numeric. Raw p-value cutoff. qvalue_cutoff Numeric. q-value cutoff. p_adjust_method Character. Multiple testing correction method. logfc_col Character. Column containing log2 fold-change values. entrez_col Character. Column containing Entrez gene identifiers. min_genes Integer. Minimum number genes required perform enrichment.

title: Run Gene Ontology enrichment analysis — run_go_enrichment
text: named list containing: go_results GO enrichment results table. ontology Ontology tested. comparison Comparison identifier. n_up_genes Number upregulated genes tested. n_down_genes Number downregulated genes tested.

what: Run sample-level RNA-seq quality control — run_sample_qc
title: Run sample-level RNA-seq quality control — run_sample_qc
text: Generate sample-level quality control outputs raw filtered count matrix, including Spearman sample correlation heatmap, hierarchical clustering dendrogram, sample correlation matrix.

title: Run sample-level RNA-seq quality control — run_sample_qc
code: run_sample_qc(   counts,   metadata,   output_dir,   annotation_cols = c("condition", "treatment", "sex", "tissue"),   dpi = 300 )

title: Run sample-level RNA-seq quality control — run_sample_qc
text: counts Numeric count matrix genes rows samples columns. metadata Sample metadata data frame. Row names must match sample names colnames(counts). output_dir Output directory QC files saved. annotation_cols Character vector metadata columns used heatmap annotations. dpi Resolution used hierarchical clustering PNG.

title: Run sample-level RNA-seq quality control — run_sample_qc
text: Invisibly returns list containing sample correlation matrix, output directory, paths generated files.

title: Run sample-level RNA-seq quality control — run_sample_qc
text: function designed used inside DEGgo workflow count filtering metadata matching. saves QC files directly provided output directory. function uses log2(count + 1) transformed counts compute Spearman sample correlations hierarchical clustering based Euclidean distance.

title: Run sample-level RNA-seq quality control — run_sample_qc
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ qc <- run_sample_qc(   counts = counts,   metadata = metadata,   output_dir = "pairwise_QC" ) } # }

title: DEGgo 0.1.0
text: Automated bulk RNA-seq quality control workflow. Differential expression analysis using DESeq2, edgeR, limma. Single-contrast pairwise analysis modes. Automated PCA, volcano plot, heatmap generation. Gene Ontology enrichment analysis visualization. Interactive HTML reporting reproducibility tracking. Support custom OrgDb annotation databases orgdb argument, enabling analyses non-model organisms.

title: DEGgo 0.1.0
text: Improved output organization reproducibility files. Enhanced DEG summary tables visualization. Added automated report generation integrated figures enrichment results.

title: DEGgo 0.0.0.9000
text: Initial development version. Added bulk RNA-seq QC, differential expression, GO enrichment, plotting helpers.

title: DEGgo 0.0.0.9000
text: Initial CRAN submission.
